You are executing a bridled pace for Job Jockey. Your task is to update documentation to reflect the tokio-based session probe.

## Docket

Update documentation to reflect tokio-based session probe.

### Context

With vosr_probe implemented in VVC (vvcp_probe.rs) and wired into saddle, documentation needs updating to reflect the new architecture.

### Deliverables

1. **JJSCSD-saddle.adoc** (`Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSD-saddle.adoc`) ‚Äî Update output format:
   - Remove or update any Needs-session-probe reference
   - Add Session-probe section to output format example:
     ```
     Session-probe:
       haiku: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
       sonnet: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
       opus: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
       host: macbook-pro.local
       platform: darwin-arm64
     ```
   - Add behavior step: "If session probe needed (gap > 1 hour), call vosr_probe and output Session-probe section"

2. **JJSA-GallopsData.adoc** (`Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc`) ‚Äî Update line ~1147:
   - OLD: "Model IDs are collected using ephemeral subagents created via --agents JSON flag..."
   - NEW: "Model IDs are collected via {vosr_probe} routine, which spawns parallel claude invocations via tokio async subprocess spawning."
   - Ensure {vosr_probe} attribute is defined if not present

### Acceptance
Both docs accurately describe the new tokio-based probe flow.

## Warrant

Agent: sonnet | Cardinality: 1 sequential | Files: Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSD-saddle.adoc, Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc (2 files)

Steps:
1. In JJSCSD-saddle.adoc add Session-probe section to output format showing 5 indented lines for haiku sonnet opus host platform
2. Add behavior step after recent-work: if session probe needed call vosr_probe and output Session-probe section
3. In JJSA-GallopsData.adoc update line 1147 to say Model IDs are collected via vosr_probe routine which spawns parallel claude invocations via tokio
Verify: grep -q vosr_probe Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run jjx_close or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Verify changes with grep to confirm vosr_probe appears in both files
2. Report completion status and verification results
3. STOP ‚Äî the calling agent will confirm wrap with the user

## Important

- Working directory is /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster
- Never use `cd` ‚Äî use absolute paths or run from project root
- Do NOT run jjx_close or wrap the pace
- Read the files first before editing to understand current content and structure

   202‚Üí== Overview
   203‚Üí
   204‚ÜíThe
   205‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   206‚Üíis the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   207‚ÜíIt tracks
   208‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   209‚Üí(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   210‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   211‚Üí(discrete actions).
   212‚ÜíEach
   213‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   214‚Üímaintains a history of
   215‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   216‚Üí(plan refinements).
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí== Design Principles
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   221‚Üí
   222‚ÜíAll access to the
   223‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   224‚Üígoes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   225‚ÜíNo jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   226‚ÜíThis is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   227‚Üí
   228‚ÜíThe rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   229‚ÜíThe internet is full of bad bash.
   230‚ÜíRust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   231‚ÜíThe compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí=== Git as Journal
   234‚Üí
   235‚ÜíThe
   236‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   237‚Üíremains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   238‚ÜíEvery mutation creates a diffable commit.
   239‚ÜíGit owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   240‚Üí
   241‚ÜíThe
   242‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   243‚Üíhistory within each
   244‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   245‚Üíexists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   246‚ÜíGit has the full history, but
   247‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   248‚Üílet us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   249‚Üí
   250‚Üí=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   251‚Üí
   252‚ÜíNew
   253‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   254‚Üíprepend to position 0 rather than append.
   255‚ÜíThis produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   256‚ÜíSmall discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   257‚Üí
   258‚ÜíThe
   259‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   260‚Üíarray contains full
   261‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   262‚ÜíEach
   263‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   264‚Üíembeds its parent
   265‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   266‚Üí(redundant within a single
   267‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}).
   268‚ÜíThis redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   271‚Üí
   272‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   273‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   274‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   275‚Üífile access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   276‚Üí{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   277‚ÜíIf {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   278‚Üí
   279‚ÜíThe pattern:
   280‚Üí[source,bash]
   281‚Üí----
   282‚Üílock_acquire
   283‚Üívvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   284‚Üílock_release
   285‚Üí----
   286‚Üí
   287‚ÜíThis keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   288‚Üí
   289‚Üí=== Deterministic Serialization
   290‚Üí
   291‚ÜíImplementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   292‚ÜíFor `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   293‚ÜíThis ensures that reordering paces via
   294‚Üí{jjdo_reorder}
   295‚Üíproduces minimal diffs ‚Äî only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí=== Identity Encoding
   298‚Üí
   299‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   300‚Üí(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
   301‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   302‚Üí(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   303‚ÜíThe base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   304‚ÜíInternally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   307‚Üí
   308‚ÜíJob Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   309‚ÜíThis is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   310‚Üí
   311‚Üí* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   312‚Üí* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   313‚Üí* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   314‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   315‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   316‚Üí* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   317‚Üí* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   318‚Üí
   319‚Üí=== Presentation Vocabulary
   320‚Üí
   321‚ÜíThe CLI and display layer uses distinct terms for certain schema fields to avoid collision with other project vocabulary:
   322‚Üí
   323‚Üí* *docket* ‚Äî the presentation term for the {jjdkm_text} field. What the {jjdpr_pace} should accomplish. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions. The JSON schema field remains `text`.
   324‚Üí* *warrant* ‚Äî the presentation term for the {jjdkm_warrant} field. Execution guidance for autonomous operation. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions. The JSON schema field remains `direction`.
   325‚Üí
   326‚ÜíThese are display/CLI terms, not schema changes. The schema field names (`text`, `direction`) are unchanged and deferred to a future heat.
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí== Upper API
   329‚Üí
   330‚ÜíJob Jockey has two distinct API layers.
   331‚ÜíThe *lower layer* is the {jjdx_cli} command set: boring, heterogeneous verb names implemented in Rust ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_close}, etc.).
   332‚ÜíThe *upper layer* is the horse racing vocabulary that humans use to direct a language model ({jjsud_slate}, {jjsum_mount}, {jjsud_wrap}, etc.).
   333‚Üí
   334‚ÜíThe separation is deliberate.
   335‚ÜíShared vocabulary between layers caused the LLM to conflate them ‚Äî guessing CLI flags by analogy, reaching for raw commands instead of following workflows.
   336‚ÜíBoring lower-layer names resist pattern matching.
   337‚ÜíVivid upper-layer names stay memorable for humans.
   338‚Üí
   339‚ÜíThe {jjsuc_insert} and {jjsus_mount} (slash command) artifacts translate between layers.
   340‚ÜíThe human speaks horse vocabulary; the LLM maps it to {jjdx_cli} invocations.
   341‚Üí

   425‚Üí=== Multistep Verbs
   426‚Üí
   427‚ÜíA {jjsum_mount} verb requires orchestration across multiple {jjdx_cli} operations.
   428‚ÜíThese are implemented as slash command skills that coordinate the workflow.

   433‚ÜíEngage the next {jjdpr_pace} for execution.
   434‚ÜíOrchestrates {jjdo_orient} to obtain context, reads the docket, executes the work, and records outcomes via {jjdo_record}.
   435‚ÜíImplemented by {jjsus_mount}.
   436‚Üí
   437‚Üí[[jjsum_groom]]

   442‚ÜíImplemented by {jjsus_groom}.
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üí[[jjsum_quarter]]
   445‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb‚üß

   450‚Üí
   451‚Üí[[jjsum_notch]]
   452‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb‚üß

   457‚Üí
   458‚Üí[[jjsum_braid]]

   540‚Üí=== Context Artifact
   541‚Üí
   542‚Üí[[jjsuc_insert]]
   543‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_section‚üß
   544‚Üí{jjsuc_insert}::
   545‚ÜíThe `MANAGED:JJK` section of the project CLAUDE.md file.
   546‚ÜíContains the quick verb mapping table, CLI command reference, and operational guidance that enables the LLM to translate upper API verbs into {jjdx_cli} invocations.
   547‚ÜíThis section is the primary mechanism by which the upper and lower API layers are bridged at runtime.
   548‚Üí
   549‚Üí== Types
   550‚Üí
   551‚Üí[[jjdt_firemark]]
   552‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   553‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}::
   554‚ÜíThe identity of a
   555‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   556‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
   557‚ÜíThe 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   558‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   559‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.
   560‚Üí
   561‚Üí[[jjdt_coronet]]
   562‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   563‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}::
   564‚ÜíThe identity of a
   565‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.
   566‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
   567‚ÜíGlobally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   568‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   569‚Üíencoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   570‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   571‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.
   572‚Üí+
   573‚Üí*Reassignment:* When a
   574‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   575‚Üíis moved between
   576‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   577‚Üívia
   578‚Üí{jjdo_relocate},
   579‚Üíthe old
   580‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   581‚Üíbecomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   582‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   583‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}.
   584‚ÜíThe new
   585‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   586‚Üíembeds the destination
   587‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   588‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   589‚Üíhistory is preserved, with a note recording the original
   590‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}.
   591‚Üí
   592‚Üí[[jjdt_silks]]

   594‚Üí{jjdt_silks}::
   595‚ÜíA kebab-case display name.
   596‚ÜíUsed for human-readable identification of
   597‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   598‚Üíand

   939‚Üí== Serialization
   940‚Üí
   941‚Üí[[jjdz_charset]]
   942‚Üí{jjdz_charset}::
   943‚ÜíThe 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   944‚ÜíUses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
   945‚Üí`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   946‚ÜíPosition in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   947‚ÜíThis alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   948‚ÜíMatches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   949‚Üí
   950‚Üí[[jjdz_encoding]]
   951‚Üí{jjdz_encoding}::
   952‚ÜíThe algorithm for encoding
   953‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   954‚Üíand
   955‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   956‚Üívalues.
   957‚Üí+
   958‚Üí*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   959‚Üí+
   960‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
   961‚Üí. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   962‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   963‚Üí+
   964‚Üí*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   965‚Üí+
   966‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
   967‚Üí. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   968‚Üí. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   969‚Üí. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   970‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   971‚Üí+
   972‚Üí*Input flexibility:*
   973‚Üí+
   974‚ÜíCLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   975‚ÜíLength determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   976‚ÜíOutput always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).

  1440‚Üí=== Steeplechase Operations
  1441‚Üí
  1442‚ÜíThe steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1443‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1444‚ÜíJSON.
  1445‚ÜíCommit messages follow patterns that
  1446‚Üí{jjdo_log}
  1447‚Üícan parse to reconstruct session history.
  1448‚Üí
  1449‚Üí==== Commit Message Patterns
  1450‚Üí
  1451‚ÜíAll JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  1452‚Üí
  1453‚Üí* `jjb` ‚Äî JJ brand prefix (literal)
  1454‚Üí* `HALLMARK` ‚Äî Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  1455‚Üí* `IDENTITY` ‚Äî ‚Ç¢CORONET for pace-level, ‚Ç£FIREMARK for heat-level
  1456‚Üí* `ACTION` ‚Äî single-letter code (required for all commits)
  1457‚Üí
  1458‚Üí*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_record}):
  1459‚Üí----
  1460‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:n: commit message
  1461‚Üí
  1462‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1463‚Üí----
  1464‚Üí
  1465‚ÜíStandard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  1466‚Üí
  1467‚Üí*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_mark}):
  1468‚Üí----
  1469‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:A: description
  1470‚Üí
  1471‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1472‚Üí----
  1473‚Üí
  1474‚Üí*Bridle marker* (created when pace transitions to bridled state):
  1475‚Üí----
  1476‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:B: {agent} | {silks}
  1477‚Üí
  1478‚Üí{full direction text}
  1479‚Üí
  1480‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1481‚Üí----
  1482‚Üí
  1483‚ÜíSubject format: `{agent} | {silks}` where agent is the designated executor and silks is the pace display name. Body contains the complete direction text that guides autonomous execution.
  1484‚Üí
  1485‚Üí*Landing marker* (created when spawned agent completes):
  1486‚Üí----
  1487‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:L: {agent} {landed|crashed}
  1488‚Üí
  1489‚Üí{agent completion report}
  1490‚Üí
  1491‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1492‚Üí----
  1493‚Üí
  1494‚ÜíSubject format: `{agent} {landed|crashed}` where agent is the executor tier (haiku/sonnet/opus) and landed/crashed indicates success/failure. Body contains the agent's raw completion report.
  1495‚Üí
  1496‚Üí*Session marker* (created automatically by {jjdo_orient}):
  1497‚Üí----
  1498‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:s: YYMMDD-HHMM session
  1499‚Üí
  1500‚Üíhaiku: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
  1501‚Üísonnet: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  1502‚Üíopus: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
  1503‚Üíhost: macbook-pro.local
  1504‚Üíplatform: darwin-arm64
  1505‚Üí
  1506‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  1507‚Üí----
  1508‚Üí
  1509‚ÜíSession markers record when a new work session begins. {jjdo_orient} creates these automatically when:
  1510‚Üí
  1511‚Üí* The most recent commit in the steeplechase is more than 1 hour old, OR
  1512‚Üí* No commits exist in the steeplechase
  1513‚Üí
  1514‚ÜíThe subject contains the session start timestamp in `YYMMDD-HHMM` format. The body contains:
  1515‚Üí
  1516‚Üí* Model IDs for haiku, sonnet, and opus tiers (full model ID strings)
  1517‚Üí* Host identifier (machine name)
  1518‚Üí* Platform string (e.g., `darwin-arm64`, `linux-x86_64`)
  1519‚Üí
  1520‚ÜíModel IDs are collected using ephemeral subagents created via `--agents` JSON flag with minimal system prompts ("Report only your exact model ID string. Nothing else."). This approach is token-cheap (~10 token prompt, ~15 token response per probe) and provides accurate version tracking for multi-device workflows.
  1521‚Üí
  1522‚ÜíPace-level action codes:
  1523‚Üí
  1524‚Üí* `n` ‚Äî Notch: standard commit
  1525‚Üí* `A` ‚Äî APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  1526‚Üí* `B` ‚Äî BRIDLE: records bridling decision before autonomous execution
  1527‚Üí* `F` ‚Äî FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  1528‚Üí* `L` ‚Äî LANDING: records agent return after autonomous execution
  1529‚Üí* `W` ‚Äî WRAP: pace completion summary
  1530‚Üí* `d` ‚Äî discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  1531‚Üí
  1532‚Üí*Heat-level operations*:
  1533‚Üí----
  1534‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:N: heat-silks
  1535‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:S: pace-silks
  1536‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:s: YYMMDD-HHMM session
  1537‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:r: reordered
  1538‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:T: pace-silks
  1539‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:D: old-coronet ‚Üí new-coronet
  1540‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:R: heat-silks
  1541‚Üí----
  1542‚Üí
  1543‚ÜíHeat-level action codes:
  1544‚Üí
  1545‚Üí* `N` ‚Äî Nominate: create new heat
  1546‚Üí* `S` ‚Äî Slate: add new pace
  1547‚Üí* `r` ‚Äî rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  1548‚Üí* `T` ‚Äî Tally: add tack to pace
  1549‚Üí* `D` ‚Äî Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  1550‚Üí* `R` ‚Äî Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  1551‚Üí
  1552‚ÜíNOTE: Officium markers migrated to VVC INVITATORY (vvb:...:i:), see VOS-VoxObscuraSpec.adoc
